development_need_canonical_name: Gross motor skills and physical confidence
stage1_why_it_matters_ux_description: Running, jumping, kicking balls, climbing stairs, and moving with increasing coordination and control.
stage2_play_idea_mapping_rationale: Ride-on toys highly recommended for 2-year-olds as they master running and directional movement.
stage3_product_name: Dolu Foot To Floor Ride-On Car - White
stage3_product_mapping_rationale: Ride-on toys highly recommended for 2-year-olds as they master running and directional movement. | Product note: Suitable for 1 year and over, foot-to-floor, push-along with storage. | Suitable for 1 year and over, foot-to-floor, push-along with storage.
optional_need_meta_stage_reason: Children at 24 months can run and kick balls; by 30 months most can jump with both feet, showing steady progression.

development_need_canonical_name: Fine motor control and hand coordination
stage1_why_it_matters_ux_description: Using both hands together for tasks, manipulating small objects, turning pages, and operating buttons and switches.
stage2_play_idea_mapping_rationale: Train sets combine construction of tracks with pretend play scenarios around age 2.
stage3_product_mapping_rationale: Train sets combine construction of tracks with pretend play scenarios around age 2. | Product note: Promotes imaginative play and fine motor skills. | Promotes imaginative play and fine motor skills.
optional_need_meta_stage_reason: At 24 months children hold containers while removing lids; by 30 months they twist doorknobs and turn pages individually.

stage1_why_it_matters_ux_description: Beginning to identify and name colors and shapes in their environment.
stage2_play_idea_mapping_rationale: Animal figures support vocabulary building and pretend play scenarios throughout toddlerhood.
stage3_product_name: Fisher-Price Little People Farm Animal Friends Figure Pack
stage3_product_mapping_rationale: Animal figures support vocabulary building and pretend play scenarios throughout toddlerhood. | Product note: Encourages imaginative play and learning about farm animals. | Encourages imaginative play and learning about farm animals.
optional_need_meta_stage_reason: Color recognition typically emerges around 30 months, making 27 months an early emerging stage for this skill.

stage1_why_it_matters_ux_description: Experiencing and expressing a wide range of emotions, developing empathy, and beginning to understand own feelings.
stage2_play_idea_mapping_rationale: Book sharing supports vocabulary growth and becomes more interactive with questions around age 2.

stage3_product_mapping_rationale: Book sharing supports vocabulary growth and becomes more interactive with questions around age 2. | Product note: Discover 50 farm sounds in this exciting book, with lots of buttons to press and animals to meet. Children will learn to recognise animals and sounds. | Discover 50 farm sounds in this exciting book, with lots of buttons to press and animals to meet. Colourful scenes with search-and-find activities inside. Children will learn to recognise animals and sounds.
optional_need_meta_stage_reason: Two-year-olds show broad emotional range but limited impulse control; empathy is emerging but self-regulation still developing.
